title: malus beauvaisiensis
intro: Malus beauvaisiensis, also known as Beauvais apple, is a species of apple tree native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots possess medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Malus beauvaisiensis include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and health.
